_doc: Managed settings for all Bubble VPS agents (Joris msg 3611, 2026-06-01). Deployed root-owned to /etc/claude-code/managed-settings.json. Per the Anthropic docs this layer CANNOT be overridden by user/project settings OR command-line args (incl. --dangerously-skip-permissions) — verified empirically: a deny rule here blocked a Read even under --dangerously-skip-permissions. Purpose: (1) the mission-file-guard PreToolUse hook lives here so an agent (or a prompt-injection) CANNOT disable it by editing user/project settings; (2) deny rules hard-block read/edit of secrets + cross-agent runtime env + the guard/settings that protect everything. NOT using allowManagedHooksOnly because each dept has its own per-path SessionStart hook that must keep loading — putting the guard in managed (un-removable) achieves the goal without breaking those. This is the Claude-Code-level layer; OS-level enforcement vs raw subprocesses comes from the sandbox (separate, follow-up piece). See ~/.claude/agent-memory/rnd/reference_vps_agent_hardening_layers.md.
